title: rubus obtusangulus var. beirensis
intro: Rubus obtusangulus var. beirensis, also known as Beire's raspberry or Beire's bramble, is a plant species within the Rubus genus, characterized by its raspberry-like appearance and thorny stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which help to expel intestinal worms, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Rubus obtusangulus var. beirensis include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
